behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure only manifests when the evaluate skill's output-routing logic hands off to the artifact store — Unit testing the skill in isolation without the store cannot confirm the artifact key used. REAL-INFRA: No real OS/network needed; an in-process fake artifact store catches the wrong key. LLM-DEP: The failure mode is about WHERE output is routed, not WHAT the LLM produces; a stubbed LLM response still exposes incorrect key routing. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected — the behavior explicitly targets artifact routing (deterministic code logic), not LLM output quality or steering. System rejected — no subprocess or real network involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the evaluate skill to a fake in-process artifact store. Invoke evaluate with a stubbed LLM response. Assert the artifact store received a write to key `grounding` and received no write to key `plan`.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure lives at the boundary between the artifact reader (fetching `grounding`) and the artifact writer (writing `plan`). A unit test of either component alone misses the round-trip. REAL-INFRA: An in-process fake store that holds both keys is sufficient. LLM-DEP: The behavior is about read/write routing, not LLM output quality; a constant stub response still reveals if implement reads the wrong key or writes to the wrong key. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected — 'reads grounding … writes to plan' describes a data-routing invariant, not an LLM decision or quality assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a fake in-process artifact store with a pre-populated `grounding` artifact. Invoke implement with a stubbed LLM. Assert the store is queried for `grounding` and that the resulting write lands on key `plan`, with `grounding` unchanged.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure requires the issue-content reader to deliver existing diagnosis AND the artifact writer to respond conditionally — Unit testing the condition alone without a live reader/writer boundary misses the real failure path. REAL-INFRA: An in-process fake issue content object and a fake artifact store are sufficient; no real OS or network required. LLM-DEP: The conditional check is on existing issue content, not LLM output; a stub LLM does not hide this failure. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected — no subprocess or real network. Agentic rejected — the skip/write decision is deterministic code logic, not LLM reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an issue content object that already carries a diagnosis field. Wire deep-dive to a fake artifact store pre-populated with a `plan` key. Invoke deep-dive. Assert the `plan` artifact in the store is unmodified after the call.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Lineage preservation requires the run-context object to persist state between two separate skill invocations — the failure only appears when a writer and a later reader share the same context object, making Unit too narrow. REAL-INFRA: An in-process run-context with an in-memory store is sufficient; the failure mode is about in-process state handoff, not filesystem or network persistence. LLM-DEP: The artifact key lookup is deterministic; a stub LLM response can populate `grounding` and the test checks that a subsequent read returns it. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected — 'same run context' implies in-process state management, not real OS I/O. Agentic rejected — lineage is a key-lookup invariant, not LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Create a shared in-process run context. Call the evaluate artifact-write path to store a value under `grounding`. Then call the implement artifact-read path. Assert the value retrieved matches what was stored, without re-fetching from any external store.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: The behavior explicitly names a three-stage sequential agent pipeline. 'Reflects grounding' asserts that LLM-generated content from the evaluate stage materially shapes LLM-generated content in the implement stage — this is an output-quality assertion across multiple real LLM calls. LLM-DEP: A stub for any stage replaces actual model judgment with a constant, making it impossible to verify that grounding propagated into and influenced the implementation output. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking any LLM step trivially passes because the assertion about content reflection becomes vacuous. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected in favor of Workflow — the behavior names three chained steps, each requiring a real model call, with the final assertion depending on end-to-end information flow across all three. Integration rejected — deterministic routing checks cannot verify that grounding content was 'reflected' in generated output.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full deep-dive → evaluate → implement pipeline against a real LLM with a synthetic issue. Capture the grounding artifact produced by evaluate. Assert that key themes or facts from the grounding artifact appear in the final `plan` artifact produced by implement.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure involves both the artifact reader (returning nothing for `grounding`) and the artifact writer (correctly writing `plan` without touching other keys) — Unit cannot cover the interaction. REAL-INFRA: An in-process fake store seeded with other artifacts (but no `grounding` key) fully reproduces the failure mode. LLM-DEP: The 'valid plan artifact' assertion can be scoped to structural validity (artifact key exists, schema correct) rather than content quality; a stubbed LLM response still exposes missing artifacts, crashes, or silent corruption of sibling keys. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected — structural validity and non-corruption of sibling artifacts are deterministic invariants, not LLM quality judgments. System rejected — no subprocess or real network required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a fake in-process artifact store with several pre-existing artifacts but no `grounding` key. Invoke implement with a stubbed LLM. Assert: (a) no exception is raised, (b) a `plan` artifact is written with valid schema, (c) all pre-existing artifacts retain their original values.
